nuggets.headline: Pete Rango is a true team player, often working with a diverse roster of artists across his projects!
nuggets.text: Beyond his own releases, Pete Rango is a prolific collaborator. He's teamed up with artists like Aaron Doh on tracks such as 'Love Hangover,' and contributed to songs with Mavi Taylor including 'Say it.' This collaborative spirit extends to his production work for artists like LIL LIL and REIN, showcasing his extensive involvement in the music creation process.

nuggets.headline: Every song on Pete Rango's 'Savage Planet' album, including 'Oms at Play,' features a special guest artist!
nuggets.text: The track 'Oms at Play' is a standout from Pete Rango's 2022 album, 'Savage Planet.' What makes this project unique is its tight-knit collaboration: all three songs on the album, including 'Oms at Play,' 'Traag Park,' and 'Planet X,' feature the distinct contributions of the artist Xmane. This consistent partnership gives 'Savage Planet' a cohesive and shared artistic vision.

nuggets.headline: If 'Oms at Play' caught your ear with its smooth, atmospheric vibe, you absolutely need to check out UMI!
nuggets.text: Pete Rango's sound, especially on 'Oms at Play,' hints at a modern blend of R&B and atmospheric production. If that resonated with you, I'd highly recommend diving into UMI's discography. Her music often features a similar chill, soulful R&B foundation, layered with lo-fi elements and a calming, introspective mood that makes for perfect, relaxed listening.

nuggets.headline: Did you know Pete Rango isn't just an artist, but a prolific producer shaping the sound of many other musicians?
nuggets.text: While Pete Rango delivers compelling tracks under his own name, he also dedicates significant time to production for others, as evidenced by a Spotify playlist entirely composed of tracks he's produced. This extensive work behind the scenes, creating sounds for artists like LIL LIL and Tangina Stone, highlights his diverse talents and deep involvement in the music landscape.

nuggets.headline: "Oms at Play" sets a significant tone for Pete Rango's "Savage Planet" album as its longest and lead track.
nuggets.text: Released in 2022, "Oms at Play" stands out not only as the opening track but also as the longest on Pete Rango's "Savage Planet" album, running for a full four minutes. This track, featuring Xmane, offers the most extended sonic journey on the three-song project, anchoring the album with its immersive atmospheric sound.

nuggets.headline: If "Oms at Play" captivated you with its smooth, atmospheric groove, you should definitely explore the dreamy R&B of Raveena.
nuggets.text: Indian-American artist Raveena Aurora, known simply as Raveena, creates a similar ethereal and soulful R&B sound, characterized by her gentle vocals and lush, ambient production. Her music, much like "Oms at Play," often evokes a sense of calm and introspection, making her a perfect discovery for those who appreciate Pete Rango's distinctive, mellow vibe.

nuggets.headline: Despite his growing presence in the music scene, Pete Rango has built his discography with a dedication typical of independent artists.
nuggets.text: Pete Rango, with his compelling tracks like 'Oms at Play,' operates with the spirit of a truly independent artist. He's steadily built a diverse catalog that includes multiple singles and EPs like 'WILD LIFE EP' and 'Dreamvibe,' alongside albums like 'Savage Planet,' showcasing a consistent commitment to his creative output.

nuggets.headline: The guest artist on 'Oms at Play' is more than just a feature; they're an actively evolving voice in today's music landscape, keeping the track fresh with their ongoing work.
nuggets.text: The captivating 'Oms at Play' features Xmane, an artist who continues to push boundaries with new material, even years after 'Savage Planet' was released. Xmane's recent releases, like the 2025 singles 'S.E.N.D.' and 'Toxicoman,' demonstrate their ongoing relevance and evolving sound, adding another layer of depth to their contribution on 'Oms at Play.'

nuggets.headline: If the laid-back, yet intricate grooves of 'Oms at Play' resonated with you, you're in for a treat with an artist who masterfully blends live instrumentation with electronic textures.
nuggets.text: Dive into the world of **FKJ**. His smooth, improvisational style, combining live instruments with electronic production, creates a lush, atmospheric soundscape that perfectly echoes the chill, soulful energy Pete Rango delivers on 'Oms at Play.' It's music designed for unwinding and getting lost in the moment.
